chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 569 of 2025 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on Misc. No.3051/2025 2. For orders on office objection No.1,14 & 19 3. For orders on Misc. No.3052/2025 4. For orders on Misc. No.3053/2025 5. For hearing of main case 13.02.2025 Mr. Ali Asghar Buriro, advocate for the petitioner 1. Granted. 2-5. The petition has been filed in the name of Owais Builders. Despite repeated queries learned counsel remained unable to demonstrate whether the petitioner was a legal entity. The petitioner assails a tender issued by the Pakistan Railways, however, learned counsel states that his instructions do not include information regarding the date when it was issued. Office objection inter alia denotes that no recourse to competent authority was availed in the first instance and also there is no demonstrable action taken for grievance resolution per the PPRA rules. Even otherwise the petition is fraught with factual controversy which is not amenable to writ jurisdiction. Petition found to be misconceived is dismissed in limine. Judge Judge Amjad
